column1: You are wearing the latest in fashion -- if you consider 1973 late!
column2: 你身上穿的可是走在時尚尖端——如果你覺得1973年也算「最新」的話！

column1: Don't give up. It's still early!
Use this icon to move your character.
column2: 點這個圖示來移動你的角色。

column1: Use this icon to look at things.
column2: 點這個圖示來查看東西。

column1: Use this icon to do things.
Use this icon to talk to other characters.
column2: 點這個圖示跟其他角色說話。

column1: Select this icon to use the current inventory object.
column2: 選這個圖示來使用目前選取的物品。

column1: Use this icon to bring up the inventory window.
column2: 點這個圖示叫出物品欄視窗。

column1: Larry! You know what this is for!
column2: 賴瑞！這個你應該知道是幹嘛的吧！

column1: This icon is for tasting and smelling.
column2: 這個圖示是用來嚐味道和聞氣味的。

column1: This icon brings up the control panel.
column2: 這個圖示會叫出控制面板。

column1: This icon tells you about other icons.
column2: 這個圖示會告訴你其他圖示的用途。

column1: Lower this if game play seems sluggish. Raise it to increase the amount of background animation.
column2: 如果遊戲跑起來卡卡的就調低；調高則會增加背景動畫的數量。

column1: This adjusts the volume on some sound boards and synthesizers.
column2: 這個用來調整部分音效卡與合成器的音量。

column1: This adjusts Larry's speed, within the limits of your computer's capabilities.
column2: 這個用來調整賴瑞的移動速度，範圍視你電腦的效能而定。

column1: Use this to save the current state of your game. When you later select Restore, everything will be exactly as it is now.
column2: 用這個來儲存目前的遊戲進度。之後選擇「讀取」時，一切都會和現在一模一樣。

column1: This restores a game you saved earlier.
column2: 這個用來讀取你先前存的進度。

column1: Use this to restart the game from the very beginning.
column2: 用這個從頭開始重玩遊戲。

column1: Use this to leave the game.
Here's where you learn more than you care to know about this game.
column2: 這裡會讓你知道一堆你根本不想知道的遊戲資訊。

column1: This helps explain the other icons.
column2: 這個用來說明其他圖示的用途。

column1: Use this to exit this menu and resume game play.
column2: 用這個離開選單、回到遊戲。

column1: The "L" in this place must be hot!
column2: 這地方的「L」字母肯定很「燙」！

column1: This is the cleanest glass in Lefty's collection.
column2: 這是老左收藏裡最乾淨的一個杯子了。

column1: Lefty's windows are too dirty to see through.
column2: 老左酒吧的窗戶髒到根本看不透。

column1: High up that telephone pole sits a lonely sign that reads, "Taxi Stand."
column2: 電線桿高處孤零零掛著一塊寫著「計程車招呼站」的牌子。

column1: It's tall and skinny; built like a pole!
column2: 又高又瘦，就跟電線桿一個模子刻出來的！

column1: You cleverly notice that light shines from only one of the windows above Lefty's. Could there be someone up there?
column2: 你眼尖地注意到老左酒吧樓上只有一扇窗透著燈光。樓上該不會有人吧？

column1: Lefty's proudly presents for your listening and drinking pleasure:
column2: 老左酒吧很自豪地為您獻上，邊喝邊聽的美好時光：

column1: Obviously, heavy drinking is not just an obsession with this guy!
column2: 很顯然，這傢伙嗜酒如命可不是說假的！

column1: She may not be a great looker, but think of the muscles she must have in that leg!
column2: 她長相普普，但那條腿的肌肉練得可扎實了！

column1: A large man sits beside the only woman in Lefty's. He's obviously thrilled with the sound of his own voice.
column2: 一個大塊頭男人坐在老左酒吧裡唯一的女人旁邊，聽自己講話聽得渾然忘我。

column1: A skinny man sits at the bar, talking to his overweight friend.
column2: 一個瘦子坐在吧台，跟他的胖朋友聊著天。

column1: An overweight man sits at the bar, talking to his skinny friend.
column2: 一個胖子坐在吧台，跟他的瘦朋友聊著天。

column1: You wonder how many naugas had to give their all just to decorate this sleazehole.
column2: 你不禁納悶，得犧牲多少「諾嘎」才能把這間破店裝潢成這樣。

column1: There is a lovely, empty, pink stool just waiting for a lovely, empty, pink rump like yours.
column2: 有張可愛的粉紅色空凳子，正等著像你這樣可愛又空空如也的粉紅屁股呢。

column1: There is a single, lovely, long-stemmed, red rose in a delicate bud vase standing incongruously on the table.
column2: 桌上突兀地擺著一只精緻花瓶，裡頭插著一朵美麗的長莖紅玫瑰。

column1: Isn't it funny how you can see the top of that table even though you're looking at it from the side?
column2: 說也奇怪，明明是從側面看，怎麼還能看到桌面呢？

column1: The door bears the scars of too many late night brawls.
column2: 這扇門上滿是深夜鬥毆留下的傷痕。

column1: You didn't know Lefty was into boxing!
column2: 你都不知道老左還有「拳擊」這一手吧！

column1: Those barrels are stacked better than any date you've ever had! But they do look as though they might fall over on top of you at any moment.
column2: 這些酒桶疊得比你談過的任何一次約會都還穩！不過看起來隨時可能倒下來砸到你。

column1: A rancid glow leaks from the next room.
column2: 隔壁房間透出一股腐臭的光。

column1: Lefty thought he ordered a "Bud Light!"
column2: 老左還以為自己點的是「百威淡啤」咧！

column1: This toilet has been provided with a handle for those customers of Lefty's sophisticated enough to understand its operation.
column2: 這馬桶特別配了拉桿，給老左酒吧裡少數幾位懂得怎麼操作的高雅顧客用。

column1: , but not interested enough to leave his post guarding the stairs.
column2: ，但還沒興趣到願意離開他鎮守的樓梯崗位。

column1: You can tell by his glazed stare, he's no rocket scientist!
column2: 從他那呆滯的眼神就看得出來，他絕對不是什麼科學家！

column1: And you thought that moosehead over Lefty's bar was stuffed!
column2: 你還以為老左吧台上那顆麋鹿頭只是標本咧！

column1: And all this time you thought that moosehead over Lefty's bar was stuffed!
column2: 這麼久以來，你都以為老左吧台上那顆麋鹿頭只是標本呢！

column1: It's the door from Lefty's bar. Its far side looks even better than this side.
column2: 這是老左酒吧的門。門的另一側看起來比這一側還順眼。

column1: That's how Lefty buys his fine wines!
column2: 老左的「上等美酒」原來都是這樣進貨的！

column1: How interesting. This door has a small peephole. You suspect it may be used to communicate with someone on the other side.
column2: 有意思，這扇門上有個小窺視孔。你猜這大概是用來跟門後的人聯絡的。

column1: The lamp casts a yellow pallor over the hooker's parlor.
column2: 那盞燈給這間妓女的房間染上一片病懨懨的黃。

column1: the small sign over the hooker's bed
column2: 妓女床頭上方的小告示牌

column1: The sign over the hooker's bed reads, "Substantial penalty for early withdrawal!"
column2: 床頭牌子上寫著：「提早退出，罰款可觀！」

column1: Someone has yet to kick the habit!
column2: 看來有人菸癮還沒戒掉！

column1: Is that all you came up here to do, Honey? LOOK!?
column2: 「親愛的，你上來就只是要『看』而已？」

column1: You think I'm pretty, doncha Honey? gums the hooker.
column2: 「你覺得人家漂亮吧，親愛的？」妓女癟著嘴含糊說。

column1: Is that why you came up here? To look at a crack?
column2: 你特地跑上來就是為了看一道「裂縫」？

column1: That's where she leaves her chewing gum overnight.
column2: 這就是她每晚黏口香糖的地方。

column1: A rickety fire escape, complete with safety ladder, is loosely attached to the building. A mild yellow glow comes from the window behind it.
column2: 一座搖搖欲墜的逃生梯連著安全梯，鬆垮垮地掛在建築物上，梯子後方的窗戶透出淡淡黃光。

column1: It appears he wants something -- YOU! Quick! Get out of here, Larry!
column2: 他顯然想要點東西——那就是你！賴瑞，快點離開這裡！

column1: You think you've seen this dumpster before!
column2: 你覺得這個垃圾子母車好眼熟！

column1: You think you've seen this fence before!
column2: 你覺得這道圍籬好眼熟！

column1: Where to, Bub? snorts the cabbie.
column2: 「要去哪，老兄？」計程車司機哼了一聲問道。

column1: Yeah, OK, but it'll cos' ja extra! the cabbie snarls.
column2: 「好啦好啦，不過要多加點錢！」司機沒好氣地說。

column1: It's not often you get to see the back of your own head, eh, Lar'?
column2: 難得能看到自己後腦勺吧，賴瑞？

column1: It seems to bear no relationship whatsoever to the rate he charges!
column2: 這東西跟他實際收的錢，好像一點關係都沒有！

column1: Isn't that unusual? Most taxicabs don't have customized license plates. You wonder if there's any special significance to the driver.
column2: 挺不尋常的，一般計程車很少掛訂製車牌。你猜這對司機來說是不是有什麼特殊意義。

column1: You wonder what the cabbie has hauled in there over the years!
column2: 你很好奇這些年司機都在後車廂裡載過什麼東西！

column1: Click the Hand here to begin a new hand of blackjack.
column2: 點這隻手來開始新一局21點。

column1: Click the Hand here to get another card.
column2: 點這隻手來多要一張牌。

column1: Click the Hand here when you want no more cards.
column2: 不想再要牌時，點這隻手。

column1: Click the Hand here to increase your bet. This button repeats automatically if you hold it down.
column2: 點這隻手來加注。按住不放會自動連續加注。

column1: Click the Hand here to decrease your bet. This button repeats automatically if you hold it down.
column2: 點這隻手來減注。按住不放會自動連續減注。

column1: Click the two arrows to increase or decrease your bet.
column2: 點兩個箭頭來增加或減少注碼。

column1: Click the Hand here to surrender your cards and have half your bet refunded.
column2: 點這隻手來棄牌投降，退回一半的注碼。

column1: Click the Hand only here when you have two cards totaling 10 or 11. You will automatically receive only one more card and double your bet.
column2: 只有手上兩張牌加起來是10或11點時，才點這隻手。系統會自動只發一張牌給你，並將注碼加倍。

column1: Click the Hand here when you have only two matching cards and you want to split them into two hands, doubling your bet.
column2: 手上兩張牌點數相同、想分成兩手牌時，點這隻手，注碼也會加倍。

column1: Click the Hand here to learn this machine's current odds.
column2: 點這隻手來查看這台機器目前的賠率。

column1: Click the Hand here to stop playing this machine. Your winnings will be given to you automatically.
column2: 點這隻手來結束這台機器，贏得的錢會自動退還給你。

column1: Click the Hand here to begin play.
Click the Hand here to increase the amount of your bet.
Click the Hand here to decrease the amount of your bet.
Click the Hand here to learn this machine's odds.
column2: 點這隻手來查看這台機器的賠率。

column1: Click the Hand here to stop playing. All the money you've deposited in the machine will be returned to you.
column2: 點這隻手來結束遊戲，你投進機器的錢都會退還給你。

column1: You hope this will soon match the other two.
column2: 你希望這個很快就能跟另外兩個湊成一樣的圖案。

column1: You must be bored to be staring at these things!
column2: 你一定是無聊到爆，才會盯著這些東西看！

column1: You are outside the lovely Lost Wages Casino and Hotel. You hear the sounds of money changing hands within. A tasteful modern sculpture decorates a fountain.
column2: 你站在美麗的失樂園賭場飯店外頭，能聽見裡頭鈔票易主的聲音。一座頗有品味的現代雕塑裝飾著噴水池。

column1: Which is more lifelike, the plant or the pot?
column2: 植物跟花盆，到底哪個比較「有生氣」？

column1: The sign above the door says "CASINO," in large, uneven letters.
column2: 門上招牌用歪七扭八的大字寫著「CASINO」。

column1: These are only a few of the millions of lights that surround this casino, you just can't see the others!
column2: 這只是這間賭場周圍上百萬盞燈的其中幾盞，其他的你根本看不完！

column1: From the David Niven, Myrna Loy School of Design.
column2: 出自「大衛尼文暨米娜洛伊設計學院」的風格。

column1: You can remember when blackjack was always played at a real table with a real dealer. Now all you see are video machines.
column2: 你還記得以前21點都是在真正的牌桌上、由真人發牌員發牌。現在放眼望去全是電動機台。

column1: You can remember when slot machines had real wheels and gears. Now all you see are video machines.
column2: 你還記得以前吃角子老虎機是靠真正的滾輪和齒輪轉動。現在放眼望去全是電動機台。

column1: What a lovely painting, although it does seem strangely familiar.
column2: 好美的一幅畫，不過總覺得莫名地眼熟。

column1: You never tire of art like this!
column2: 這種藝術品，你怎麼看都不會膩！

column1: Peering through the casino, you can see the next room and a lovely planter filled with colorful plants.
column2: 你越過賭場往裡瞧，能看到隔壁房間，還有一座擺滿繽紛植物的漂亮花架。

column1: You just love these colorful plastic plants.
column2: 你就是喜歡這些五顏六色的塑膠植物。

column1: You just love that planter filled with plastic plants.
column2: 你就是喜歡那座擺滿塑膠植物的花架。

column1: Exciting video slot machines await your money.
column2: 刺激的電動吃角子老虎機，正等著吃掉你的錢。

column1: The latest state-of-the-gambling-art video blackjack machines seem to draw your billfold from your pocket directly to their yawning maws!
column2: 這些最新型的電動21點機台，簡直能把你口袋裡的皮夾直接吸進它們張大的血盆大口！

column1: What wonderful decor! You just love furniture with a sense of humor.
column2: 真是絕妙的裝潢！你就是愛這種有幽默感的家具。

column1: That elevator must be made by the DNA Company.
column2: 這台電梯肯定是「DNA公司」造的。

column1: Why, it appears some sort of card is lying in the ashtray.
column2: 咦，菸灰缸裡好像躺著一張卡片。

column1: Is that a window or an illusory painting? With art like this, who can tell?
column2: 那是窗戶還是幻視畫？這種藝術水準，誰分得出來？

column1: Oh, no! Look! Someone has painted the word "Cabaret" right there on the wall.
column2: 天啊！你看！有人在牆上寫了「歌舞秀場」這幾個字。

column1: The hotel has stuck a cheap plastic heart to the door with a thumbtack. That's all it takes to create a Honeymoon Suite!
column2: 飯店用一根圖釘把一顆廉價塑膠愛心釘在門上，就這樣一間「蜜月套房」誕生了！

column1: the door with the heart on it
Now that you're no longer staring at Faith's ample weapons, you notice an unlabeled button on her desk.
column2: 你總算不再盯著費絲那對「傲人武器」看了，這才注意到她桌上有顆沒標示的按鈕。

column1: A beautiful security guard sits at the desk. A pair of large pistols are in a holster around her slim waist. She looks friendly, but quick on the trigger.
column2: 一位美麗的保全小姐坐在櫃台後，纖細的腰間繫著槍套，插著兩把大手槍。她看起來很和善，但扣扳機肯定也很快。

column1: Atop the elevator shaft, a modern woman runs to meet her alien lover.
column2: 電梯井頂端，一位現代女子正跑向她的外星愛人。

column1: Hurry, Larry! You're losing your new buddy!
column2: 快點，賴瑞！你的新朋友要不見了！

column1: The brass nameplate at the bottom says the title of this work of art is, "Right."
column2: 底下的銅牌寫著這件作品名叫《右》。

column1: The brass nameplate at the bottom says the title of this work of art is, "Left."
column2: 底下的銅牌寫著這件作品名叫《左》。

column1: This sofa couldn't possibly be comfortable.
column2: 這張沙發坐起來絕不可能舒服。

column1: Your eyes are immediately attracted to the sculptures in the planter.
column2: 你的目光立刻被花架裡那些雕像吸引過去。

column1: What the hell are those two sculptures planning to do!?
column2: 那兩座雕像到底想幹嘛！？

column1: Be careful you don't bump your shin on THAT table!
column2: 小心別讓小腿撞到那張桌子！

column1: A shelf with pots and a plant sit above the elevator.
column2: 電梯上方有個架子，擺著花盆和植物。

column1: Looking through the skylight, you see sky.
column2: 透過天窗，你看到的是天空。

column1: Looking through the doorway, you see the skyline of downtown Lost Wages, spread out before you like a cheap trick.
column2: 透過那扇門望去，失樂園市中心的天際線就這麼廉價地攤在你眼前。

column1: Outside that doorway is the penthouse's rooftop patio.
column2: 那扇門外是頂樓套房的露天平台。

column1: The Casino Hotel's Penthouse Suite's bedroom is lavishly appointed with the latest in Bill Skirvin art.
column2: 這間賭場飯店頂樓套房的臥室，用比爾·斯克文的最新畫作豪華裝飾著。

column1: It appears to be an inflatable doll.
column2: 看起來像是個充氣娃娃。

column1: Oh, look! A portrait of Jesse Helms!
column2: 你看！一幅傑西·赫姆斯的肖像畫！

column1: Oh, that's disgusting. Be glad you don't have a higher-resolution screen!
column2: 噁，真噁心。慶幸你的螢幕解析度沒有更高吧！

column1: Play your cards right and you might get to try this baby out tonight!
column2: 牌打得好的話，說不定今晚就能試試這寶貝了！

column1: They look soft and fluffy -- just like their owner!
column2: 看起來又軟又蓬——跟主人一個樣！

column1: What an unusual plant! But how can it grow near all those lights?
column2: 好特別的植物！不過在那堆燈光旁邊是要怎麼長大啊？

column1: This tree is the only normal-looking plant around. That's because it's artificial!
column2: 這棵樹是這附近唯一看起來正常的植物——因為它是假的！

column1: Her mouth reminds you of something.
column2: 她的嘴讓你聯想到某樣東西。

column1: You used this to inflate half of her.
column2: 你就是用這個把她吹了一半氣。

column1: At this particular moment, you feel her right breast is the best.
column2: 此時此刻，你覺得她的右胸最棒。

column1: At this particular moment, you feel her left breast is the best.
column2: 此時此刻，你覺得她的左胸最棒。

column1: Behind her rests an expensive fluffy bath towel.
column2: 她身後放著一條看起來很貴的蓬鬆浴巾。

column1: It's a good thing polyester doesn't wrinkle.
There's some sort of control button built into the surface of the spa's decking.
column2: SPA池邊的甲板上嵌著某種控制按鈕。
你看！竿子上那對「貓頭鷹」！

column1: The hot tub looks so warm and inviting.
column2: 這座按摩浴缸看起來溫暖誘人。

column1: You were in that very same living room only moments ago.
column2: 你才剛從那間客廳過來沒多久。

column1: A lovely waterfall cascades down lava rockwork through a mass of exotic flora.
column2: 一道美麗的瀑布順著熔岩造型的岩石，穿過一片奇花異草傾瀉而下。

column1: Her eyes are a deep loamy brown.
column2: 她的眼睛是深邃的泥土棕色。

column1: There's a control button built into the surface of the spa's decking.
column2: SPA池邊的甲板上嵌著一顆控制按鈕。

column1: It's pretty complicated. There's a knob that says "Power."
column2: 這東西挺複雜的，上面有個寫著「電源」的旋鈕。

column1: You love the thought of a heart-shaped bed!
column2: 一想到心型床，你就整個人融化了！

column1: Out the window the lights of Lost Wages spread before you like some cheap trick.
column2: 窗外失樂園的萬家燈火，廉價地在你眼前展開。

column1: To you, it looks like dozens of spermatozoa attacking a Whitman's sampler!
column2: 在你看來，這簡直像數十隻精子在圍攻一盒惠特曼巧克力！

column1: How sweet. Fawn has placed the rose you gave her in that vase. What a romantic she is!
column2: 真貼心，芳恩把你送的那朵玫瑰插進了那只花瓶。她真是個浪漫的女人！

column1: You can just picture Fawn using that chair in the morning.
column2: 你可以想見芳恩早上坐在那張椅子上的模樣。

column1: How clever! A painting of the opposite side of the room. Many hotels would just install a mirror.
column2: 真聰明！用一幅畫來呈現房間對面的樣子，換作一般飯店大概就直接裝面鏡子了事。

column1: Wait a minute! What are those sculptures doing?!
column2: 等等！那些雕像到底在幹嘛！？

column1: You are outside Lost Wages' finest instant marriage center, the "Quiki-Wed" Wedding Chapel, another in the vast conglomerate that has become the fabulous "Quiki" empire. An interesting form resides beneath that light pole.
column2: 你站在失樂園最頂級的閃婚中心「快快婚」教堂外頭，這是龐大的「快快」帝國旗下的又一家分店。路燈下站著一個有意思的身影。

column1: Through these doors walk the finest suckers in the world!
column2: 「走進這扇門的，都是全世界最好騙的冤大頭！」

column1: Between the Casino and the Quiki-Wed Chapel a lovely fountain gurgles gently in this good night. Above it, a pair of cherubs frolic in a tasteful sculpture.
column2: 在賭場和快快婚教堂之間，一座美麗的噴水池在這美好的夜裡潺潺作響，上方一對小天使雕像正玩鬧著，頗有品味。

column1: Most of that fountain is in the next scene.
column2: 那座噴水池大部分都在下一個場景裡。

column1: How romantic -- service with a smile!
column2: 真浪漫——服務還附贈笑容！
真浪漫——「已服務超過十億對」！

column1: You are in a quickie marriage parlor. A flashing neon sign on the rear wall asks: "Why wait? Marry the girl of your dreams today! You provide the girl, we'll do the rest for only $100!!"
column2: 你人在一間閃婚小教堂裡。後牆上一塊霓虹燈閃爍著：「何必等待？今天就迎娶夢中情人！你只要負責帶女生來，剩下的我們只收你100元搞定！！」

column1: He looks a lot like his brother, that bad comedian in the Lizard Lounge.
column2: 他長得很像他哥——就是那個在蜥蜴酒廊講爛笑話的諧星。

column1: It seems everything here is covered in plastic.
column2: 這裡似乎每樣東西都包著一層塑膠。

column1: A lovely artificial bouquet resides about an artificial mantle above an artificial fireplace /altar.
column2: 一束美麗的人造花束擺在人造壁爐（祭壇）上方的人造壁爐架上。

column1: If you don't look too closely, you can barely see the wires running up the inside of the PVC tubing.
column2: 只要不仔細看，你幾乎看不出PVC管子裡藏著電線。

column1: You are outside Lost Wages' one and only Quiki-Mart Convenience Store. A telephone hangs from the utility pole, minus its directory.
column2: 你站在失樂園唯一一家快快超商外頭。電線桿上掛著一具電話，電話簿早就不知去向。

column1: The lights above the doorway illuminate the entrance to the Quiki-Mart store.
column2: 門口上方的燈照亮了快快超商的入口。

column1: You're not sure, but you think you hear cries of "Help" coming from that dark alley over there.
column2: 你不太確定，不過你好像聽到那條暗巷傳來「救命」的呼喊聲。

column1: When you look hard enough, you think you can just barely make out the outline of a car speeding toward you.
column2: 你努力瞇眼一看，隱約能看出一輛車正朝你疾駛而來的輪廓。

column1: This window looks perfectly rectangular -- to you!
column2: 這扇窗在你看來完美地呈長方形！

column1: This sign indicates you are currently in front of the lovely Lost Wages Quiki-Mart.
column2: 這塊招牌顯示你目前站在美麗的失樂園快快超商前。

column1: The street light casts its yellow glow across the area, thoughtfully illuminating the many pieces of trash that line this thoroughfare.
column2: 路燈灑下一片黃光，貼心地照亮了這條大街兩旁滿地的垃圾。

column1: The Lost Wages Art Emporium will satisfy your every need for modern art, as long as you're looking for something with dead fish!
column2: 失樂園藝術商場能滿足你對現代藝術的一切需求——只要你要找的是有死魚的那種！

column1: The bum looks like a man that could (ab)use a drink.
column2: 這遊民看起來就像個能把酒「喝好喝滿」的人。

column1: You are using the Quiki-Mart's one and only telephone.
column2: 你正在使用快快超商唯一的那具電話。

column1: So tell me, Larry: what is the best part of your body?
column2: 「那告訴我，賴瑞，你身上哪個部位最棒？」

column1: And Larry, what is your favorite X-rated video?
column2: 「賴瑞，那你最喜歡的限制級影片是哪一部？」

column1: Now tell me your favorite article of clothing.
column2: 「現在告訴我你最喜歡的一件衣服。」

column1: Larry, what is your favorite sex partner's first name?
column2: 「賴瑞，你最喜歡的床伴叫什麼名字？」

column1: What's the best part of %s's anatomy?
What is %s's sexiest article of clothing?
And where do you two most enjoy making love?
column2: 「那你們兩個最喜歡在哪裡做愛？」

column1: And, finally, what do you and %s like to do when you're together?
column2: 「最後，你和%s在一起時最喜歡做什麼？」

column1: Thoroughly inspecting the touchtone telephone, you conclude that it's just like any other telephone.
column2: 仔細檢查這具按鍵電話後，你得出結論：它跟其他電話沒兩樣。

column1: You are in a 24-hour convenience store. There is a magazine rack near the front door, with a sign reading "This no riblaly -- no leeding." Except for the clerk standing behind the counter, the store appears to be deserted. A small sign graces the counter.
column2: 你人在一間24小時營業的便利商店裡。門口附近有座雜誌架，上頭立著一塊牌子，寫著「此不是圖書館－勿佔看」。除了櫃台後面站著的店員外，店裡似乎空無一人。櫃台上擺著一塊小告示牌。

column1: What law says that convenience store clerks must be non-English speaking?!
column2: 到底哪條法律規定超商店員都聽不懂人話啊？！

column1: That extra large box of wine looks like a bargain.
column2: 那盒特大號的盒裝酒看起來挺划算的。

column1: There's nothing of interest inside the freezer.
column2: 冷凍櫃裡沒什麼有趣的東西。

column1: the rack filled with breath spray
column2: 擺滿口氣清新噴劑的架子

column1: The counter contains a lovely display rack filled with breath spray. And, it's your brand!
column2: 櫃台上擺著一個裝滿口氣清新噴劑的展示架，而且還是你愛用的牌子！

column1: You may want to warm something in this microwave oven, but you can't.
column2: 你或許想用這台微波爐熱點東西，可惜辦不到。
那塊笨手笨腳做的小招牌

column1: Why doesn't that dog use this fireplug instead of you?
column2: 那隻狗幹嘛不找消防栓尿尿，偏偏找上你？

column1: You are outside a flashy disco with an unhappy bouncer.
column2: 你站在一間浮誇的迪斯可舞廳外，門口的保鑣一臉不爽。

column1: The disco is filled with remarkably similar guys, all looking for remarkably similar girls.
column2: 舞廳裡擠滿了長得都差不多的男生，全都在找長得都差不多的女生。

column1: What a great idea! Live fish in an aquarium.
column2: 好主意！水族箱裡養活魚。

column1: It looks like the best seat in the house.
column2: 看起來是這裡最好的位子。

column1: You wonder if Patti knows Bill is hanging around with Al Lowe again!
column2: 你在想Patti知不知道Bill又跟Al Lowe混在一起了！

column1: A few fish swim around in the gigantic aquarium.
column2: 幾隻魚在這座巨大的水族箱裡游來游去。

column1: Kevin is that handsome gent near the divider.
Barry Smith thought he could hide out here, instead of drawing for a living!
column2: Barry Smith還以為可以躲在這裡，不用靠畫畫混飯吃！

column1: Mike feels good like a programmer should.
column2: Mike爽快，工程師就該這麼爽快。

column1: Oliver Brelsford's neck must be getting stiff by now, staring across the room.
column2: Oliver Brelsford這樣一直盯著房間對面看，脖子大概都僵了。

column1: Have you ever seen Roger in a chair high enough so his legs weren't scrambled?
column2: 你有看過Roger坐在一張夠高的椅子上、雙腿不會被擠成一團的樣子嗎？

column1: It's not often you see coral used for a room divider.
column2: 用珊瑚當隔屏，還真是少見。

column1: Her lips are moist and inviting, but remember: you also thought that about old Mrs. Bradley, your homeroom teacher!
column2: 她的嘴唇濕潤誘人，不過別忘了：你當年也是這樣覺得你的導師布萊德利老太太的！

column1: High up that pole sits a lonely sign that reads, "Taxi Stand."
column2: 電線桿高處孤零零掛著一塊寫著「計程車招呼站」的牌子。

column1: To look more closely at an object, first click here, then click on the item.
column2: 想仔細看一樣物品，先點這裡，再點該物品。

column1: Use this when you want to do something to an item.
column2: 想對某樣物品做動作時，用這個。

column1: To use an item in the game, first click here, then click on the item, then click on OK.
column2: 要在遊戲中使用某樣物品，先點這裡，再點該物品，最後點「確定」。

column1: Click here to close this window and return to the game.
column2: 點這裡關閉視窗，回到遊戲。

column1: Select the game that you would like to restore.
column2: 選擇你想要讀取的存檔。

column1: Type the description of this saved game.
column2: 輸入這個存檔的描述文字。

column1: This directory/disk can hold no more saved games. You must replace one of your saved games or use Change Directory to save on a different directory/disk.
column2: 這個目錄/磁碟已經沒有空間存放更多存檔了。你必須覆蓋其中一個存檔，或用「切換目錄」存到別的目錄/磁碟。
最大可用空間：%u 位元組
